_README: TEMPLATE — copy to config.<client>-<product>-<market>.json and fill every <...> and TO_RECORD_* value. This tool is client-agnostic: nothing is product-specific until you fill this in. Run `build_keyword_workbook.py --config <your config> --preflight` to see what inputs are still missing. See NEW-CLIENT.md.

product_anchor.asin: <anchor ASIN, must be a DataDive-tracked column in the master CSV>
product_anchor.asin_note: <optional: ASIN gotchas, e.g. a pack/variant that resolves to a different product>

product_facts._comment: Physical product facts from the LABEL/PDP. Fill BEFORE writing seo_content — they gate the title framing (blend vs single) and compliance. ingredient NAMES are factual (allowed); ingredient EFFECTS need an EU register entry at dose (see eu-compliance-matrix.md). The builder warns if this block is missing and checks it when present.

ingredients.name: <ingredient name as it may appear in copy>

datadive_exports._comment: Metadata for BOTH MKL exports. Placeholder values (TO_RECORD_*) FAIL validation by design — fill them at export time.

related_niche_filter._comment: STRICT relevance gate for the POE Raw - Related Niches tab. keep = ONLY niches genuinely relevant to this product. exclude_examples = known drift the build must drop (validation fails if any survive). Fill from the actual POE related-niches grid.

triage._comment: Token rules for Outlier triage. Fill brand_tokens from the DataDive competitors; form/claim/negative for THIS product form + marketplace language.
form_tokens: <wrong-form word for this product, e.g. capsule/drink/serum>
claim_tokens: <health/claim-risk word for this category — see eu-compliance-matrix.md>

semantic_opportunity._comment: When no token rule fires, flag as opportunity if relevant AND the anchor product under-ranks vs competitors. (Legacy key names are still accepted for back-compat.)

compliance._comment: OPTIONAL health-claims compliance layer — see skills/amazon-seo/references/health-claims-compliance.md. category_tier: regulated (supplements/foods-with-claims/health&beauty/medical-adjacent) or standard (household/general). regime: EU or US. risk_posture: standard (kill HIGH only in regulated; keep MEDIUM when it ranks) | conservative | aggressive. Set checked=true after the health-claims self-check ran for this listing. claims_audit: per-term verdicts from a self-check or an Amazon SAS audit; verdict=prohibited terms are merged into triage.claim_tokens at build time.

root_importance._comment: '1. Root Keywords' is written as: Important | Root Keyword | Frequency | Broad Search Volume | Root Score | Category. The Important column is the AD-TARGETING signal: with ad_min_sv/ad_min_score set (tiered mode), roots get the ad_marker when score >= ad_min_score AND Broad SV >= ad_min_sv AND Category is not Brand/Claim/Form/Off-niche — these seed SKW/rank campaigns. Roots with score >= min_score but no SV floor get the plain marker. Category comes from the same triage tokens + core/POE vocabulary as the Never-Ever ladder. Legacy configs with only min_score/marker keep the old binary marking.

never_ever._comment: Generated from the Expanded MKL 1% file into a SECTIONED audit tab: (1) Never Ever single-word negatives (apply as negative phrase) with Category + Why + SV/relevancy evidence + example phrases; (2) competitor brands (campaign-dependent: negative in rank/SKW, target in PAT/conquest — never blanket negatives); (3) claim-risk words (compliance review); (4) review-manually near-miss band (relevancy between the auto cut and review_band_max_relevancy); (5) phrase-level negative candidates (combination-irrelevant phrases whose words are individually clean, top 30 by SV; disable with phrase_candidates=false). relevant_words protect real product intent from being auto-negated; explicit_never_words force-negate known junk.
relevant_words: <core product words to NEVER mark negative>

campaign_structure._comment: OPTIONAL — Campaign Structure fill (visual PPC plan in the '5. Campaign Structure' tab; see fill_campaign_structure.py and the amazon-seo-keyword-workflow skill). Thresholds + naming live LOCAL-ONLY in _local/ads-strategy/ (copy ads-strategy.TEMPLATE.{json,md}); never put them here.
own_brand_tokens: <own brand words/phrases as they appear in search terms>
campaign_structure.product_name_for_naming: <SHORT product name used in campaign names, e.g. 'Manuka Balm'>

tabs._comment: Production model. The template .xlsx is STYLE ONLY — no product-specific tab is carried forward. exact_paste = 1:1 CSV (exception: the roots CSV gets a header-cleanup transform — see root_importance); rebuild = generated from current sources; generated_blank = explicit skipped row.
tabs._carry_forward_comment: 5. Campaign Structure is carried forward as the empty PPC scaffold from template_keyword_workbook.xlsx (Rank/Shield SKW waves, Long-Tails, Discovery, PAT) so keywords can be filled in later. Do NOT add it to generated_blank — that would wipe the scaffold. Leave generated_blank empty unless a tab genuinely needs an explicit skipped row.

inputs._comment: Input contract — paths the builder reads (CLI flags still override; ~ and repo-relative resolved). Fill all of these for the new client.
inputs.template: <path to a styled .xlsx to reuse as style base>

stale_data_guard._comment: Fails the build if a previous product's/market's terms leak into the guarded tabs (the bug where an Italian fibre workbook inherited German collagen review topics). Fill forbidden_terms with the PREVIOUS template product's distinctive terms.
forbidden_terms: <distinctive term from the template's product that must NOT appear>
